value: As of 2026-04-22 evening main is at 5828aac — PR #149 merged: pairing.expired journal events wired (ccsc-rc1 closed). Audit EventKind coverage 18/19. Test count 694 / 4,029 expects. Coverage 98.46% line / 98.82% func. Backlog: 3 P3s remaining — ccsc-scv (pairing.accepted design: file-watcher vs IPC vs server-side pairing; split from ccsc-rc1 this session), ccsc-0mn (Stryker CI — needs 3 stable baselines), ccsc-4nm (MCP tool-completion — externally blocked). Lesson from #149 review cycle: Gemini review can reverse itself across rounds (round-2 🟡 'separate filter from delete', round-3 🟡 'merge them back into one loop' on the same function). Correct response is to dismiss reviewer-churn once the code is in a reasonable state, not ping-pong. Three commits on the branch tell the story: initial + two accepted suggestions + one dismissed. Repo clean, main only, stale epic32b stash unchanged.

/beads CTO mode: when user runs /beads or asks what's next, act as CTO — read bd ready, pick the next right thing toward production-grade scalable software, and ASSIGN it (delegate to specialized Agent for audits/research/specialization-heavy work, or claim myself for P0 invariants and security gates). Announce decision, run 'bd update <id> --status in_progress', start. Don't list queues and wait — Jeremy hired Claude to make the call. Goal: international software standards, scales, serves real people.

Jeremy is a truck driver using Claude to build production-grade software. He cannot supervise at the technical level. Operate with full engineering autonomy: pick the bead, make the design call, claim or delegate, execute. NO trailing confirmation questions ('want me to X or Y?'). Surface decisions only for product/priority/money/legal/user-facing calls — otherwise execute and inform.

v0.7.0 shipped Epic 30-B: per-channel audit projection to Slack threads. ChannelPolicy now has audit field ('off'|'compact'|'full'). Pre-execution receipts posted to originating thread. auditReceipts Map capped at 500 entries for memory safety. Self-echo regression tests locked in (PR #108). Closed issues #29 (policy engine), #30 (threaded action journal), #97 (user_id binding) — all shipped. Next: Epic 31 (bot-manifest protocol, 32 ready beads starting with ccsc-s53.4 P0 invariant).
